title: The Complete Phone Durability Guide: Everything You Know About Cases and Screen Protectors Is Wrong
excerpt: That $60 case you bought might be making your phone more likely to break. New engineering research reveals how billion-dollar marketing campaigns have convinced us to protect our phones in ways that actually make them more vulnerable. I spent six months testing this theory and the results will change how you think about phone protection.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Online Safety in 2026
excerpt: I got my bank account drained by clicking a single malicious link last year. Here's everything I learned about staying safe online, including the security tools that actually work and the ones that are just expensive placebos.

title: How Hackers Actually Break Into Phones - And How to Stop Them
excerpt: Last month, I watched a security researcher crack into an iPhone 15 Pro in under 20 minutes. It wasn't some Hollywood movie magic - just three simple techniques that most people have never heard of. Here's what I learned about how phone hacking actually works.

title: Your Old iPhone Is Killing Kids in Ghana (And Apple Knows It)
excerpt: That phone you 'responsibly recycled' last year? There's a 70% chance it's now poisoning children in Agbogbloshie—the world's largest e-waste dump. I tracked where our devices really go, and the truth is worse than you think.

title: Faceless YouTube Channels: How They Work and What to Expect
excerpt: Not everyone wants to be on camera, and faceless YouTube channels are a real alternative. From compilation channels to narrated explainers, anonymous creators are building audiences and earning revenue. Here is how they do it.

title: Making Money Blogging: Why Most People Quit and How to Stick With It
excerpt: Everyone selling you blogging dreams skips the part where most people quit before seeing a single dollar. Here's what actually happens in those brutal first months, from someone who learned the hard way.
